segments.text: Introduction to network configuration in cloud computing; comparison of tools in AWS and Azure.
The importance of networking alongside storage and computation for hosting secure and scalable websites.
The necessity of private networks and subnetting to separate public-facing websites from secure databases and payment systems.
Defining AWS VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) and Azure VNet as tools for creating isolated, scalable networks.
How VPC and VNet ensure resources communicate privately and scale according to demand.
Introduction to CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) as the foundation for IP allocation in the cloud.
The critical role of subnetting in dividing internal networks based on specific organizational requirements.
Managing inbound and outbound traffic through Internet Gateways and NAT Gateways.
Security implementation using Security Groups, NACLs, or NSGs to block vulnerabilities and DOS attacks.
Handling remote access and communication between regions or data centers using VPNs and private connections.
The use of routing tables to direct data packets within the network infrastructure.
Conclusion and reference to future detailed videos on CIDR and subnetting logic.
